input.title: Healthcare Patient Portal
input.description: A HIPAA-compliant web portal that enables patients to manage their healthcare experience online. Patients can register, book appointments with providers, access medical records and lab results, manage prescriptions, communicate securely with care teams, pay bills, and verify insurance coverage. The system integrates with existing Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems via HL7 FHIR APIs.

user_classes.Patient: Registered individual who accesses their health records, books appointments, communicates with providers, manages prescriptions, and pays medical bills through the portal.
user_classes.Provider: Licensed physician, nurse practitioner, or specialist who manages their schedule, reviews patient records, responds to messages, and writes prescriptions.
user_classes.Admin: Healthcare facility administrator who manages users, provider schedules, billing configuration, insurance integrations, and compliance settings.
user_classes.Lab Technician: Clinical staff who upload lab results and attach them to patient records for provider review and patient access.

definitions.PHI: Protected Health Information — any individually identifiable health information that is subject to HIPAA privacy and security rules.
definitions.EHR: Electronic Health Record — the digital system of record for patient health data, integrated via FHIR APIs.
definitions.FHIR: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources — the standard API specification for exchanging healthcare data between systems.
definitions.Encounter: A documented interaction between a patient and provider, including visit notes, diagnoses, and treatment plans.
definitions.Formulary: The list of prescription medications covered by a patient's insurance plan.

scope: Patient self-registration with identity verification and HIPAA consent
Appointment booking with provider search, availability calendar, and visit type selection
Read access to medical records including encounter notes, diagnoses, allergies, and immunizations
Prescription viewing, refill requests, and pharmacy selection
HIPAA-compliant secure messaging between patients and care teams
Lab result viewing with abnormal value flagging and provider annotations
Online bill pay with itemized statements and insurance adjustment breakdowns
Automated appointment reminders via email, SMS, and in-app notifications
Real-time insurance eligibility verification via payer APIs
Emergency contact and advance directive management
out_of_scope: Provider-side clinical documentation and order entry
Clinical decision support and drug interaction checking
constraints: All PHI must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+) per HIPAA Security Rule
Audit logs must record every access to PHI with user ID, timestamp, action, and resource accessed
Session timeout must enforce automatic logout after 15 minutes of inactivity
Multi-factor authentication is required for all patient and provider accounts
The portal must integrate with existing EHR systems via HL7 FHIR R4 APIs
Password requirements: minimum 12 characters, upper, lower, digit, special character
Data retention must comply with state-specific medical record retention laws (minimum 7 years)

operating_environment.frontend: Angular SPA deployed behind a WAF (Web Application Firewall) on a HIPAA-compliant hosting provider.
operating_environment.backend: Spring Boot REST API deployed on AWS ECS with HIPAA BAA. Spring Security for authentication and authorization.
operating_environment.database: PostgreSQL on AWS RDS with encryption at rest enabled. Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for PHI columns.
operating_environment.integration: HL7 FHIR R4 endpoints for EHR integration. Payer eligibility APIs for insurance verification. Stripe for payment processing with HIPAA-compliant configuration.

FR-01.title: Patient Registration and Identity Verification
requirements: Patients register with full legal name, date of birth, email, phone, and a government-issued ID upload for identity verification
Email and phone are verified via OTP before account activation
Password must meet HIPAA-strength requirements: 12+ characters with upper, lower, digit, and special character
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is mandatory — patients choose between SMS OTP or authenticator app (TOTP)
Patient must review and digitally sign HIPAA consent and Notice of Privacy Practices during registration
Identity verification is reviewed and approved by admin before full portal access is granted
Patients can designate authorized representatives (e.g., parents for minors) with documented consent

FR-02.title: Appointment Booking
requirements: Patients can search for providers by name, specialty, location, and available dates
Provider profiles show name, credentials, specialties, bio, accepted insurance plans, and patient ratings
Available appointment slots are displayed in a calendar view based on provider schedule and visit type duration
Visit types include: new patient, follow-up, annual physical, urgent care, and specialist consultation
Patients select a slot, provide a reason for visit, and confirm the booking
Booking confirmation is sent via email and SMS with calendar attachment (ICS file)
Patients can reschedule or cancel appointments up to 24 hours before the scheduled time
No-show tracking: patients with 3+ no-shows in 12 months see a warning and may have booking restrictions

FR-03.title: Medical Records Access
requirements: Patients can view their medical record summary including: active diagnoses, allergies, medications, immunizations, and surgical history
Encounter history shows past visits with date, provider name, reason, diagnoses, and visit notes
Each record entry displays the data source (portal, EHR import, lab) and last updated timestamp
Patients can download their complete medical record as a FHIR-compliant JSON bundle or a human-readable PDF
Record access is restricted to the authenticated patient and their authorized representatives
Every access to medical records is logged in the HIPAA audit trail

FR-04.title: Prescription Management
requirements: Patients can view all current and past prescriptions with medication name, dosage, frequency, prescribing provider, and refills remaining
Patients can request a prescription refill for eligible medications (refills remaining > 0 and not expired)
Refill requests are sent to the prescribing provider for approval
Patients can select or change their preferred pharmacy from a searchable list
Approved refills are sent electronically to the selected pharmacy via e-prescribing integration
Patients receive a notification when a refill is approved, denied, or ready for pickup
Controlled substance prescriptions require additional provider verification before refill approval

FR-05.title: Secure Messaging
requirements: Patients can send secure messages to their assigned care team (primary provider, nurse, care coordinator)
Messages support text, file attachments (images, PDFs up to 10MB), and urgency level (routine, urgent)
Providers or care team members can respond from the provider portal
Messages are encrypted end-to-end and stored with AES-256 encryption at rest
Auto-reply is sent when a message is received outside of business hours with expected response time
Urgent messages trigger an alert notification to the provider's mobile device
Message threads are retained for the full medical record retention period (7+ years)

FR-06.title: Lab Results Viewing
requirements: Lab results are imported from the lab system or EHR via FHIR APIs and appear on the patient's portal
Results display test name, value, reference range, units, and abnormal flag (high, low, critical)
Abnormal values are highlighted in red with a severity indicator
Providers can attach annotations or explanatory notes to lab results before patient release
A configurable hold period (default 3 days) allows providers to review results before patient access
Patients receive a notification when new lab results are available
Historical results for the same test type can be viewed as a trend chart over time

FR-07.title: Billing and Payments
requirements: Patients can view itemized billing statements with procedure codes, charges, insurance adjustments, and patient responsibility
Outstanding balances are aggregated into a single 'Amount Due' with due date
Patients can pay online via credit card or bank account (ACH) through Stripe
Payment plans can be set up for balances over a configurable threshold (e.g., $500)
Payment receipts are generated and available for download as PDF
Patients can view payment history with date, amount, method, and receipt download
Insurance explanation of benefits (EOB) documents are viewable alongside billing statements

FR-08.title: Appointment Reminders and Notifications
requirements: Automated appointment reminders are sent 48 hours and 2 hours before the scheduled time
Reminders are delivered via email, SMS, and in-app notification based on patient preferences
Reminder includes date, time, provider name, location, and preparation instructions
Patients can confirm or cancel directly from the reminder notification
Notifications are also sent for: new messages, lab results available, prescription updates, and billing due dates
Notification preferences allow patients to enable or disable each channel and type independently

FR-09.title: Insurance Eligibility Verification
requirements: Patients enter insurance information: payer name, member ID, group number, and upload card images
Real-time eligibility check is performed against the payer's API at booking time and at check-in
Verification returns: active/inactive status, coverage type, copay amounts, deductible remaining, and prior authorization requirements
Ineligible or expired insurance shows a clear alert with guidance on next steps
Insurance information is stored securely and linked to the patient's billing profile
Admin can configure payer API connections and mapping for each supported insurance provider

FR-10.title: Emergency Contacts and Advance Directives
requirements: Patients can manage a list of emergency contacts with name, relationship, phone, and priority order
At least one emergency contact is required to complete the patient profile
Patients can upload advance directive documents (living will, healthcare power of attorney) as PDF
Advance directives are flagged prominently on the patient's medical record for provider visibility
Patients can update or revoke advance directives with a date-stamped audit trail
Emergency contact information is accessible to providers during urgent care encounters

NFR-01.title: HIPAA Compliance
requirements: All PHI is encrypted at rest with AES-256 and in transit with TLS 1.2+
Comprehensive audit logs track every PHI access, modification, and disclosure
Session timeout after 15 minutes of inactivity with automatic logout
Annual security risk assessment documentation is supported by the system

NFR-02.title: Performance
requirements: Portal pages must load within 3 seconds including data fetch
Appointment availability calendar must return within 2 seconds
Insurance eligibility check must complete within 5 seconds

NFR-03.title: Availability
requirements: 99.9% uptime SLA for the patient portal
Scheduled maintenance windows must be communicated 72 hours in advance
Automatic failover to a standby instance within 60 seconds

system_attributes.reliability: Database replication with automatic failover. Transaction logging for all billing and clinical operations.
system_attributes.scalability: Stateless Spring Boot instances behind a load balancer. Read replicas for medical record queries.
system_attributes.security: HIPAA-compliant infrastructure with BAA. Column-level encryption for PHI. Role-based access control with audit logging. WAF protection against OWASP Top 10.
system_attributes.maintainability: Spring Boot actuator for health monitoring. Flyway for database migrations. Centralized logging with ELK stack.

FR-01.title: Build Patient Registration API with Identity Verification
FR-01.description: Create POST /api/patients/register accepting: fullName, dateOfBirth, email, phone, password, and governmentIdImage (file upload). Validate password strength (12+ chars, upper, lower, digit, special). Send OTP to both email and phone for dual verification. Store the patient with status 'pending_verification'. After OTP confirmation, change status to 'pending_identity_review'. Admin reviews uploaded ID and approves or rejects. Create POST /api/patients/register/consent for recording HIPAA consent and NPP digital signature with timestamp.
acceptance_criteria: Registration validates password meets all HIPAA requirements.
Both email and phone OTP must be verified before proceeding.
Government ID is stored in encrypted cloud storage, not in the database.
HIPAA consent records the signed timestamp and IP address.
Account remains restricted until admin approves identity verification.
Password hash uses bcrypt with cost factor 12 or higher.

FR-01.title: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication
FR-01.description: Build MFA enrollment during registration: patient chooses SMS OTP or TOTP authenticator app. For TOTP, generate a secret key and display a QR code for scanning with Google Authenticator or similar. On every login after password verification, require the second factor. Create Spring Security filter that intercepts login and enforces MFA before issuing the session token. Support backup codes (10 single-use codes) for account recovery.
acceptance_criteria: MFA is required on every login — no bypass option.
TOTP QR code is generated and scannable by standard authenticator apps.
SMS OTP is sent via the configured SMS provider and expires in 5 minutes.
Backup codes are generated, hashed, and each can only be used once.
Failed MFA attempts are logged and trigger lockout after 5 failures.

FR-01.title: Build Registration and Login UI
FR-01.description: Implement Angular pages: multi-step registration form (personal info → password → ID upload → OTP verification → HIPAA consent with full text and checkbox → MFA setup with QR code or SMS choice). Login page with email/password form, MFA prompt screen (TOTP input or SMS code), and backup code entry option. Session timeout warning dialog appearing at 13 minutes with 'Stay Logged In' button. Automatic logout at 15 minutes with redirect to login.
acceptance_criteria: Registration stepper validates each step before allowing progression.
ID upload accepts JPEG/PNG with preview and reupload option.
HIPAA consent displays full legal text with mandatory checkbox.
MFA setup shows QR code with copyable secret key as fallback.
Session timeout warning appears at 13 minutes with countdown.

FR-01.title: Build Authorized Representative Management
FR-01.description: Create CRUD endpoints for /api/patients/me/representatives: POST (add representative with name, relationship, email, phone, consent document upload), GET (list), DELETE (revoke). Representatives must accept an email invitation and complete their own registration with MFA. When active, representatives can access the patient's portal with a 'viewing as representative' banner. All representative access is logged separately in the audit trail.
acceptance_criteria: Representative invitation requires consent document upload.
Representatives must register with MFA before getting access.
Representative access is clearly labeled in the UI and audit logs.
Revoking a representative immediately terminates their access.
Audit trail distinguishes patient access from representative access.

FR-01.title: Create Patients and Auth Schema
FR-01.description: Define JPA entities: Patient (id UUID, fullName (encrypted), dateOfBirth (encrypted), email (encrypted, unique), phone (encrypted, unique), passwordHash, status ENUM(pending_verification/pending_identity_review/active/suspended), mfaMethod ENUM(sms/totp), mfaSecret (encrypted), backupCodes (encrypted JSON), hipaaConsentSignedAt, consentIpAddress, governmentIdStorageKey, createdAt, updatedAt). AuthorizedRepresentative (id, patientId FK, name, relationship, email, status, consentDocKey, createdAt). Create Flyway migrations with column-level encryption for all PHI fields.
acceptance_criteria: All PHI columns use AES-256 encryption via JPA attribute converters.
Email and phone have unique constraints on their encrypted values.
MFA secret is encrypted at rest — never stored in plaintext.
Flyway migration applies encryption to existing data if migrating.
Indexes work with encrypted columns via hash-based lookup columns.

FR-01.title: Implement HIPAA Audit Logging
FR-01.description: Build a Spring AOP aspect that intercepts all API calls accessing PHI. Log to a dedicated audit_log table: userId, userRole, action (view/create/update/delete), resourceType (patient/record/message/lab), resourceId, ipAddress, userAgent, timestamp. Audit logs are append-only — no updates or deletes allowed. Create GET /api/admin/audit-logs with filtering by user, resource, action, and date range. Audit logs are retained indefinitely (minimum 7 years per policy).
acceptance_criteria: Every PHI access generates an audit log entry automatically.
Audit logs cannot be modified or deleted (append-only table).
Admin can search and filter audit logs by user, resource, and date.
Audit log includes IP address and user agent for forensics.
Database user for audit table has INSERT-only permissions.

FR-01.title: Write Registration and Auth Tests
FR-01.description: Test registration with valid data, password strength validation (all rules), email and phone OTP verification, identity verification admin approval and rejection, MFA enrollment (TOTP and SMS), MFA login enforcement, backup code usage, session timeout at 15 minutes, representative invitation and access, HIPAA consent recording, and audit log generation for all auth events.
acceptance_criteria: Password tests cover all HIPAA requirements individually.
MFA tests verify both TOTP and SMS pathways.
Session timeout test confirms logout at exactly 15 minutes.
Audit tests verify log entries for registration, login, and MFA events.

FR-02.title: Build Provider Search and Availability API
FR-02.description: Create GET /api/providers with filters: name (partial match), specialty, location (zip code with radius), acceptedInsurance, and availableDate. Each provider returns: name, credentials, specialties, bio, photo, accepted insurance list, and average patient rating. Create GET /api/providers/:id/availability?visitType=&startDate=&endDate= that returns available 15/30/60-minute slots based on the provider's schedule template minus booked appointments. Visit type durations: new patient (60min), follow-up (30min), annual physical (60min), urgent (30min), specialist (45min).
acceptance_criteria: Provider search returns results matching all applied filters.
Availability excludes already-booked slots and blocked time.
Slot duration varies by visit type (30, 45, or 60 minutes).
Availability spans provider's configured working hours only.
Search by zip code returns providers within a configurable radius.

FR-02.title: Build Appointment Booking API
FR-02.description: Create POST /api/appointments accepting: providerId, slotDateTime, visitType, reasonForVisit. Validate slot is still available (prevent double-booking with optimistic locking). Create the appointment with status 'confirmed'. Send confirmation via email (with ICS attachment) and SMS. Create PATCH /api/appointments/:id for rescheduling (only if ≥24h before appointment). Create DELETE /api/appointments/:id for cancellation (only if ≥24h before). Track no-shows per patient and enforce booking restrictions after 3 no-shows in 12 months.
acceptance_criteria: Double-booking the same slot returns 409 Conflict.
Confirmation email includes an ICS calendar attachment.
Rescheduling within 24 hours of appointment returns 400.
Cancellation within 24 hours of appointment returns 400.
Patient with 3+ no-shows sees a warning and has restricted booking.
All booking actions are logged in the audit trail.

FR-02.title: Build Appointment Booking UI
FR-02.description: Implement Angular pages: provider search with filters sidebar (specialty dropdown, insurance multiselect, location input), provider cards with photo, credentials, and rating. Provider detail page with bio and availability calendar. Calendar shows available slots color-coded by visit type. Booking flow: select slot → enter reason → confirm. My appointments page showing upcoming and past appointments with reschedule/cancel buttons (disabled within 24h). No-show warning banner for patients near the threshold.
acceptance_criteria: Provider search filters update results dynamically.
Calendar shows only available slots for the selected visit type.
Booking confirmation shows a summary with edit option before submitting.
Reschedule/cancel buttons are disabled within 24 hours of appointment.
No-show warning banner is prominent for patients with 2+ no-shows.

FR-02.title: Create Appointments and Providers Schema
FR-02.description: Define JPA entities: Provider (id, userId FK, specialties JSON, credentials, bio, photoUrl, acceptedInsurancePlans JSON, locationZip, avgRating). ProviderSchedule (id, providerId FK, dayOfWeek, startTime, endTime, slotDurationMinutes). Appointment (id, patientId FK, providerId FK, slotDateTime, endDateTime, visitType ENUM, reasonForVisit, status ENUM(confirmed/rescheduled/cancelled/completed/no_show), createdAt). Create index on (providerId, slotDateTime) for availability queries. Create Flyway migrations.
acceptance_criteria: Compound index on (providerId, slotDateTime) prevents double-booking.
Provider schedule supports different hours per day of week.
Appointment status tracks the full lifecycle including no-shows.
reasonForVisit is encrypted as it may contain PHI.

FR-02.title: Write Appointment Booking Tests
FR-02.description: Test provider search with all filter combinations, availability calculation (working hours minus booked), slot booking with optimistic locking (concurrent booking test), confirmation email with ICS, rescheduling within and outside 24-hour window, cancellation rules, no-show tracking and booking restriction enforcement, and audit logging for all booking actions.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent booking test confirms only one succeeds for the same slot.
Availability tests verify correct slots based on schedule and existing bookings.
24-hour restriction tests verify enforcement for both reschedule and cancel.
No-show restriction test confirms booking blocked after 3 incidents.

FR-03.title: Build Medical Records API with FHIR Integration
FR-03.description: Create GET /api/patients/me/medical-record returning a summary: activeDiagnoses (ICD-10 code, description, diagnosedDate), allergies (substance, reaction, severity), medications (name, dosage, frequency, prescriber), immunizations (vaccine, date, lot number), and surgicalHistory (procedure, date, provider). Data is aggregated from the local database and the EHR system via FHIR Patient/$summary operation. Create GET /api/patients/me/encounters (paginated list of past visits). Create GET /api/patients/me/medical-record/export?format=fhir|pdf for download.
acceptance_criteria: Medical summary aggregates data from local DB and EHR FHIR API.
Each record entry shows its data source (portal, EHR, lab).
FHIR export returns a valid FHIR R4 Bundle in JSON format.
PDF export generates a human-readable document with all sections.
Access is restricted to the authenticated patient and authorized representatives.
Every record access generates a HIPAA audit log entry.

FR-03.title: Build Medical Records UI
FR-03.description: Implement the Angular medical records page: tabbed view with sections for Diagnoses, Allergies, Medications, Immunizations, and Surgical History. Each section shows a table with sortable columns. Encounter history shows a timeline of past visits with expandable details (provider, reason, diagnoses, notes). Export buttons for FHIR JSON and PDF downloads. Provider name and data source badge on each entry. Last updated timestamp on each section.
acceptance_criteria: All five record sections display with correct data and sorting.
Encounter timeline shows visits in reverse chronological order.
Export buttons generate downloads in the selected format.
Data source badges distinguish local, EHR, and lab origins.
Loading states show while fetching from EHR integration.

FR-03.title: Configure EHR FHIR Integration
FR-03.description: Implement a Spring Boot FHIR client using HAPI FHIR library to connect to the facility's EHR system. Configure OAuth2 client credentials flow for FHIR API authentication. Map FHIR resources (Patient, Condition, AllergyIntolerance, MedicationStatement, Immunization, Procedure, Encounter) to portal domain models. Implement caching (Redis, 15-minute TTL) to reduce EHR API call volume. Handle EHR downtime with graceful degradation (show local data with 'EHR unavailable' warning).
acceptance_criteria: FHIR client authenticates with OAuth2 and retrieves patient resources.
All required FHIR resource types are mapped to portal models.
Cached responses reduce EHR API calls for repeated requests.
EHR downtime shows local data with a degradation warning.
FHIR API errors are logged and do not crash the portal.

FR-03.title: Write Medical Records Tests
FR-03.description: Test medical summary aggregation from local and FHIR sources, encounter history pagination, FHIR export bundle validity, PDF export content completeness, access control (own records only, representative access), audit log generation on every access, and EHR integration degradation when FHIR API is unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Aggregation tests verify merged data from local DB and FHIR mock.
FHIR export test validates the output against FHIR R4 schema.
Access control tests verify patient isolation and representative access.
Degradation test confirms portal functions with local data when EHR is down.

FR-04.title: Build Prescription Management API
FR-04.description: Create GET /api/patients/me/prescriptions returning current and past prescriptions (medication name, dosage, frequency, prescriberId, pharmacyId, refillsRemaining, expirationDate, status, lastFilledDate). Create POST /api/prescriptions/:id/refill-request validating refills remaining > 0, not expired, and not a controlled substance (or flag for additional verification). Refill requests go to the provider's queue. Create PATCH /api/prescriptions/:id/refill-response (provider: approve or deny with reason). On approval, send e-prescribing message to the selected pharmacy.
acceptance_criteria: Prescription list includes both active and historical prescriptions.
Refill request validates eligibility (refills remaining, not expired).
Controlled substance refills are flagged for additional provider review.
Approved refills trigger e-prescribing to the patient's pharmacy.
Provider can deny with a reason that is visible to the patient.
All prescription access is audit-logged.

FR-04.title: Build Pharmacy Selection API
FR-04.description: Create GET /api/pharmacies?zip=&name= that searches pharmacies by location or name. Create PATCH /api/patients/me/pharmacy to set or change the preferred pharmacy. Pharmacy data includes: name, address, phone, hours, and supported e-prescribing network. When a patient changes their pharmacy, all future refills route to the new pharmacy.
acceptance_criteria: Pharmacy search returns results by zip code proximity or name match.
Setting a preferred pharmacy updates the patient's profile.
Pharmacy details include operating hours and network support.
Pharmacy change applies to all future refill approvals.

FR-04.title: Build Prescription Management UI
FR-04.description: Implement the Angular prescriptions page: current medications table with name, dosage, frequency, refills remaining, and actions column. 'Request Refill' button (disabled if no refills or expired). Refill request confirmation dialog showing medication details and pharmacy. Past prescriptions tab showing historical data. Pharmacy selector with search, current pharmacy display, and change button. Notification banners for approved/denied refills. Controlled substance badge on applicable medications.
acceptance_criteria: Current medications table shows all active prescriptions with status.
Refill button is disabled with tooltip explaining why when ineligible.
Controlled substance badge is visually distinct with an info tooltip.
Pharmacy selector shows current pharmacy and allows search for alternatives.
Notification banner appears for refill status updates.

FR-04.title: Configure E-Prescribing Integration
FR-04.description: Set up integration with the Surescripts or equivalent e-prescribing network. Implement the NCPDP SCRIPT standard for sending prescription messages to pharmacies. Configure API credentials and endpoint routing. Handle pharmacy not-found and network errors with fallback to fax notification. Log all e-prescribing transactions for compliance auditing.
acceptance_criteria: Approved refills are transmitted electronically to the pharmacy.
Transmission failures fall back to fax or provider notification.
All e-prescribing transactions are logged with timestamps and status.
Controlled substance transmissions include required DEA identifiers.
Network errors do not block the provider's workflow.

FR-04.title: Write Prescription Management Tests
FR-04.description: Test prescription listing (active and historical), refill request validation (eligible, no refills, expired, controlled substance), provider approval and denial, e-prescribing transmission on approval, pharmacy selection and change, controlled substance additional verification, and audit logging for all prescription access.
acceptance_criteria: Refill eligibility tests cover all validation rules.
Controlled substance tests verify additional verification flow.
E-prescribing tests verify transmission with mock pharmacy endpoint.
Audit tests verify log entries for view, request, and approval actions.

FR-05.title: Build Secure Messaging API
FR-05.description: Create POST /api/messages with recipientId (provider or care team member), subject, body, urgencyLevel (routine/urgent), and optional file attachment (max 10MB, stored encrypted in S3). Messages are encrypted with AES-256 before database storage. Create GET /api/messages/threads (list conversations) and GET /api/messages/threads/:id (full thread). Implement auto-reply for messages received outside business hours. Urgent messages trigger a push notification to the provider. All message access is audit-logged.
acceptance_criteria: Message body is encrypted at rest with AES-256.
File attachments are encrypted in S3 with signed download URLs.
Urgent messages trigger push notification to the provider.
Auto-reply is sent for messages outside business hours with expected response time.
Message access by either party is audit-logged.
Messages are retained for the full retention period (7+ years).

FR-05.title: Build Secure Messaging UI
FR-05.description: Implement the Angular messaging interface: inbox view with threads sorted by most recent, showing recipient name, subject, last message preview, urgency badge, and unread indicator. Thread detail view with message bubbles, timestamps, and attachment download links. Compose form with recipient selector (care team members), subject, rich text body, urgency toggle, and file attachment upload. Auto-reply notice displayed when messaging outside business hours. Unread count badge in the navigation.
acceptance_criteria: Inbox sorts threads by most recent message with unread indicators.
Urgent messages show a red urgency badge in the inbox.
File attachments show filename and size with download button.
Compose form validates recipient and subject before enabling send.
Auto-reply notice is informational and does not block sending.

FR-05.title: Write Secure Messaging Tests
FR-05.description: Test message sending and receiving, encryption at rest verification (verify stored data is not plaintext), file attachment upload and encrypted download, urgency-based notification triggering, auto-reply for out-of-hours messages, message retention, thread pagination, and audit logging for all message access.
acceptance_criteria: Encryption test verifies database-stored message body is ciphertext.
Attachment test verifies encrypted storage and signed URL access.
Urgency test verifies push notification for urgent messages only.
Audit test verifies log entries for send, read, and attachment download.

FR-06.title: Build Lab Results API with Hold Period
FR-06.description: Create an internal endpoint POST /api/lab-results (used by lab system integration) to import results: patientId, testName, testCode (LOINC), value, units, referenceRange, abnormalFlag (normal/high/low/critical), collectedDate, reportedDate. Results are created with status 'pending_review' and a configurable hold period (default 3 days). Providers can add annotations via PATCH /api/lab-results/:id/annotate and release early via POST /api/lab-results/:id/release. After hold period or early release, status changes to 'available' and patient is notified. Create GET /api/patients/me/lab-results and GET /api/patients/me/lab-results/trend?testCode= for historical chart data.
acceptance_criteria: Lab results are held for the configured period before patient access.
Provider can annotate with explanatory notes before release.
Provider can release results early if reviewed.
Patient is notified when results become available.
Trend API returns historical values for the same LOINC code.
Abnormal values are flagged with severity level.
All lab result access is audit-logged.

FR-06.title: Build Lab Results UI
FR-06.description: Implement the Angular lab results page: results table with test name, value, reference range, abnormal flag (color-coded: green normal, orange high/low, red critical), date, and provider annotation. Filter by date range and test category. Trend chart view for a selected test showing historical values with the reference range as shaded bands. Abnormal values have tooltip explanations. New results badge notification. Table supports sorting by date, test name, and abnormal status.
acceptance_criteria: Abnormal values are color-coded: green normal, orange high/low, red critical.
Trend chart shows historical values with reference range bands.
Provider annotations appear as expandable notes below the result.
New (unread) results show a badge indicator.
Table sorting and date filtering work correctly.

FR-06.title: Configure Lab System FHIR Integration
FR-06.description: Implement a FHIR listener endpoint that receives DiagnosticReport and Observation resources from the lab system via FHIR subscription or batch import. Map LOINC codes to display-friendly test names. Handle result updates (amended results) by versioning. Configure the FHIR endpoint with OAuth2 authentication. Implement retry logic for failed imports.
acceptance_criteria: FHIR DiagnosticReport and Observation resources are correctly mapped.
LOINC codes resolve to human-readable test names.
Amended results update the existing record with version history.
FHIR endpoint authenticates incoming requests with OAuth2.
Failed imports are retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff.

FR-06.title: Write Lab Results Tests
FR-06.description: Test result import and hold period enforcement, provider annotation and early release, patient notification on availability, abnormal flag display, trend data calculation for historical values, FHIR resource mapping, amended result versioning, and audit logging for all result access.
acceptance_criteria: Hold period test confirms results are hidden from patient during hold.
Early release test confirms immediate patient access after provider action.
Trend test verifies correct data points for historical chart.
Amended result test verifies version history is maintained.

FR-07.title: Build Billing and Payments API
FR-07.description: Create GET /api/patients/me/billing/statements (paginated list of billing statements with procedureCode, description, charges, insuranceAdjustments, patientResponsibility, status). Create GET /api/patients/me/billing/balance returning aggregated amount due with due date. Create POST /api/patients/me/billing/pay with Stripe PaymentIntent for card or ACH. Create POST /api/patients/me/billing/payment-plan for balances over threshold — configure installment amount and frequency. Generate PDF receipts on successful payment. Create GET /api/patients/me/billing/history for payment records with receipt downloads.
acceptance_criteria: Statements show itemized charges with insurance adjustments.
Balance aggregates all outstanding patient responsibility amounts.
Payment creates a Stripe PaymentIntent with the correct amount.
Payment plans split the balance into scheduled installments.
PDF receipt is generated and stored for each successful payment.
All billing access is audit-logged.

FR-07.title: Build Billing and Payments UI
FR-07.description: Implement Angular billing pages: billing summary showing amount due with pay button, itemized statements table with expandable rows showing charges/adjustments/EOB, payment form with saved cards and add-new-card option, payment plan setup wizard for large balances, payment history table with receipt PDF download links. EOB documents displayed alongside corresponding billing statements. Amount-due banner appears on dashboard when balance is overdue.
acceptance_criteria: Billing summary prominently shows amount due and due date.
Itemized view expands to show individual charges and adjustments.
Payment form with Stripe Elements validates card input.
Payment plan wizard shows installment schedule before confirmation.
Receipt PDFs download correctly from payment history.

FR-07.title: Write Billing and Payment Tests
FR-07.description: Test statement retrieval with itemized charges, balance calculation across multiple statements, payment with card and ACH, payment plan creation and installment scheduling, refund processing, PDF receipt generation, payment history pagination, and EOB document linking.
acceptance_criteria: Balance tests verify correct aggregation across statements.
Payment tests verify Stripe PaymentIntent with mock.
Payment plan tests verify installment schedule calculation.
Receipt tests verify PDF generation with correct transaction details.

FR-08.title: Build Appointment Reminder System
FR-08.description: Implement a Spring Boot scheduled task that runs every 15 minutes checking for appointments due for 48-hour and 2-hour reminders. Send reminders via the patient's preferred channels (email, SMS, in-app push). Email includes date, time, provider, location, and preparation instructions specific to the visit type. Include confirm/cancel deep links in email and SMS. Track reminder delivery status. Create GET/PATCH /api/patients/me/notification-preferences for managing channel and type preferences.
acceptance_criteria: 48-hour and 2-hour reminders are sent at the correct times.
Reminders respect patient channel preferences (email, SMS, push).
Confirm deep link updates appointment status to 'confirmed'.
Cancel deep link initiates cancellation if within the allowed window.
Reminders are not sent for already-cancelled appointments.
Visit-type-specific preparation instructions are included.

FR-08.title: Build Notifications Center UI
FR-08.description: Implement the Angular notification center accessible from the header bell icon with unread badge. Notification feed showing all notifications grouped by today, this week, and earlier. Each notification shows icon, title, body, timestamp, and action link. Notification preferences page with toggle matrix: rows for notification types (appointments, messages, lab results, prescriptions, billing) and columns for channels (email, SMS, in-app). Mark-as-read on click and mark-all-as-read button.
acceptance_criteria: Unread badge shows correct count and updates in real time.
Notification feed groups by time period with expandable sections.
Clicking a notification navigates to the relevant page.
Preferences toggle matrix persists changes immediately.
Mark-all-as-read clears the unread badge.

FR-08.title: Write Notification and Reminder Tests
FR-08.description: Test 48-hour and 2-hour reminder scheduling, channel preference enforcement (email only, SMS only, all channels), confirm/cancel deep link handling, cancelled appointment reminder suppression, notification preferences CRUD, notification feed pagination, and unread count accuracy.
acceptance_criteria: Reminder timing tests verify correct scheduling for both windows.
Channel tests verify delivery only to enabled channels.
Deep link tests verify confirm and cancel actions.
Preference tests verify channel/type matrix persistence.

FR-09.title: Build Insurance Verification API
FR-09.description: Create POST /api/patients/me/insurance accepting payerName, memberId, groupNumber, and card images (front/back). Store insurance data encrypted. Create POST /api/insurance/verify-eligibility that calls the payer's eligibility API (X12 270/271 or REST) with patient demographics and member ID. Returns: eligibilityStatus, coverageType, copayAmounts (by service type), deductibleRemaining, priorAuthRequired flag. Cache eligibility results in Redis for 24 hours. Run automatic eligibility check 24 hours before scheduled appointments.
acceptance_criteria: Insurance data is stored with AES-256 encryption.
Eligibility check returns structured coverage details.
Ineligible or expired insurance returns a clear status with guidance.
Results are cached for 24 hours to reduce payer API calls.
Pre-appointment eligibility check runs automatically.
Insurance card images are stored encrypted in cloud storage.

FR-09.title: Build Insurance Management UI
FR-09.description: Implement Angular insurance pages: insurance card upload (front/back with camera capture or file upload), insurance details form (payer dropdown, member ID, group number), eligibility status display showing coverage summary with copay, deductible, and authorization requirements. Alert banner for expired or ineligible insurance. Link to contact support for insurance issues. Admin page for configuring payer API connections.
acceptance_criteria: Card upload supports camera and file with image preview.
Eligibility result shows a clear summary with copay and deductible.
Ineligible insurance shows an alert with next-step guidance.
Form validates required fields before allowing eligibility check.

FR-09.title: Configure Payer Eligibility API Integration
FR-09.description: Set up integration with payer eligibility APIs (X12 270/271 transactions or RESTful equivalents). Configure API credentials per payer. Map X12 270 request format from patient demographics. Parse X12 271 response into structured eligibility data. Handle payer-specific response variations and error codes. Implement connection health monitoring and alerting for payer API outages.
acceptance_criteria: X12 270 request is generated correctly from patient data.
X12 271 response is parsed into structured eligibility fields.
Payer-specific variations are handled via configurable mappings.
API outages trigger alerts and show graceful error to users.
Admin can configure and test payer connections.

FR-09.title: Write Insurance Verification Tests
FR-09.description: Test insurance data storage and encryption, eligibility check with active coverage, expired coverage, and payer API errors. Test pre-appointment automatic verification, eligibility caching, card image upload and storage, and admin payer configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Eligibility tests cover active, expired, and error scenarios.
Encryption tests verify insurance data is stored as ciphertext.
Caching tests verify Redis storage and TTL enforcement.
Pre-appointment test confirms automatic check 24 hours before.

FR-10.title: Build Emergency Contacts and Advance Directives API
FR-10.description: Create CRUD endpoints for /api/patients/me/emergency-contacts (name, relationship, phone, email, priority order). Validate at least one contact exists in the patient profile completeness check. Create POST /api/patients/me/advance-directives for uploading PDF documents with type (living_will, healthcare_poa, dnr) and effectiveDate. Create PATCH for updating and DELETE for revoking (soft delete with revocation timestamp). All changes are date-stamped in an audit trail. Create a provider-facing GET endpoint that flags patients with active advance directives.
acceptance_criteria: At least one emergency contact is required for profile completeness.
Contacts maintain priority ordering for emergency notification.
Advance directives are stored as encrypted PDFs in cloud storage.
Revocation creates a timestamped audit entry and soft-deletes the document.
Provider endpoint flags patients with active advance directives.
All changes are audit-logged with date, time, and user ID.

FR-10.title: Build Emergency Contacts and Directives UI
FR-10.description: Implement Angular pages: emergency contacts list with add/edit/delete and drag-to-reorder priority. Each contact card shows name, relationship, phone with click-to-call link. Required contact validation warning if none exist. Advance directives section with document type tabs (Living Will, Healthcare POA, DNR), upload with PDF preview, effective date picker, and revoke button with confirmation dialog. Document history showing all versions with upload and revocation dates.
acceptance_criteria: Contact list allows drag-to-reorder for priority assignment.
Missing emergency contact shows a warning banner.
Advance directive upload shows PDF preview before saving.
Revoke button shows confirmation with irreversibility warning.
Document history shows full version timeline with dates.

FR-10.title: Write Emergency Contacts and Directives Tests
FR-10.description: Test emergency contact CRUD, priority ordering, minimum-one-contact validation, advance directive upload and storage encryption, revocation with audit trail, provider-facing directive flag, document version history, and access control (patient-only modification, provider read-only).
acceptance_criteria: Minimum contact tests verify profile completeness enforcement.
Directive tests verify encrypted storage and signed URL access.
Revocation tests verify soft delete and audit trail entry.
Access control tests verify patient vs. provider permissions.
